title: quercus glauca subsp. annulata
intro: Quercus glauca subsp. annulata, also known as Japanese evergreen oak, is a subspecies of tree native to Japan and parts of Asia, characterized by its glaucous leaves and distinctive growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus glauca subsp. annulata are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as arthritis and high blood pressure. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in herbal supplements or as a tea, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
